landmarks.headingPreview: Taxis to and from JFK Airport

sections.textPreview: Taxi pick-ups and drop-offs may take longer than usual due to construction at JFK. Plan accordingly and allow for extra time. During this period we recommend taking Public Transportation to the airport.

Before you drive off, make sure you won't get ripped offIf you accept a ride from a fraudulent driver you will get ripped off and scammed. Go to the official pick up areas for authorized vehicles such as yellow cabs, Ubers or Lyfts.

sections.heading: Taxi fares between JFK Airport and Manhattan
sections.textPreview: Taxi fares between JFK Airport and ManhattanFor your convenience, taxis from JFK to Manhattan have a flat rate of $70, plus tolls, tip, and $2.75-$8.50 in additional fees which vary by time of day and destinations. The meter and receipt will show this fixed price. Flat Fare Polic
Note: Meter must read $3.00 at the start of the trip (except for JFK-Manhattan $70 Flat Fare trips).
The Bronx
Riverdale $110 South Bronx $80
Brooklyn
Coney Island $70 Downtown Brooklyn $90 East New York $40 Williamsburg $80
Queens
Corona and Forest Hills $40 Flushing $60 Long Island City and Astoria $70 Ozone Park $30
Staten Island
Eltingville, Annadale, and Prince’s Bay $130 Saint George and New Brighton $100
Nassau/Westchester, NY
The fare shown on the meter from JFK to NYC boundary, then double the fare from there to final stop.
Connecticut/Suffolk/New Jersey/north and west of Westchester County
Price is negotiated between passenger and driver at the start of the trip.
Review our guide on transferring airports for more information on connecting to flights at LaGuardia and Newark Liberty International Airports.

ctas.text: Review our guide on transferring airports

sections.textPreview: LaGuardia Airport
Newark Liberty International Airport
Tipping is optional but appreciated and encouraged.
One fare pays for all passengers going to the same place.
4 passengers is the limit for NYC cabs (5 for minivans).
Don’t forget to take your receipt before you go!

sections.heading: Accessible NYC taxis
sections.textPreview: Accessible NYC taxisYou can request a wheelchair-accessible taxi through the Accessible Dispatch program opens in a new tab or by using apps like Curb opens in a new tab or ARRO opens in a new tab. You can also call or text the dispatch service directly: Call: +1 (646)-599-9999 T

sections.heading: Taxi commission information
sections.textPreview: Taxi commission informationTaxis are regulated by the NYC Taxi and Limousine Commission. For taxi compliments, complaints and lost property, call 311 or visit the NYC Taxi & Limousine Commission website. opens in a new tab
